meta.title: Give And Take
meta.productType: Student-to-Investor life-path board game with QR web companion and session-code backend
meta.designStyle: Modern board-game finance: illustrated 6000px background under vector gameplay layers, navy/gold/category-accent hierarchy, bundled Inter typography, readable school prototype
meta.evidenceBoundary: AI simulation verifies mechanics and balance; real student surveys verify target-audience understanding.

prototypeContract.movement: Roll one six-sided die and move forward on the linear S00-S43 path.
prototypeContract.endCondition: Score when all players reach S43 Finish Review or after 12 turns per player, whichever happens first.
prototypeContract.dashboardRole: QR web app can run a playable session with guest/account access, join-by-session-code, setup, turns, decks, prices, player ledgers, scoring, and evidence export; it falls back to browser-local mode when the session backend is unavailable; physical player boards remain the fallback.

responsibilities: Roll one six-sided die on their turn.
Move one pawn disc or cube forward on S00-S43.
Manage fictional cash, holdings, evidence notes, ethics, and reflection on the Player Board.
Record one decision, finance term, or evidence note each turn.
Maintain the shared asset price tracker.
Read and apply Market/Life, Ethics, Action, and Reflection card effects.
Use the QR web app when available; physical tracking remains valid.
Score the game using the current formula.

playerRoles.starterProfiles: Starter Profiles give players asymmetric starting cash and one small rule modifier. They are player setup cards, not separate character classes with extra hidden mechanics.

boardSpec.printPageSize: 506mm x 506mm including 3mm bleed on every edge
boardSpec.safeMargin: Keep critical text, icons, QR replacement art, pawn pads, and gameplay markings at least 10mm inside the final trim.
boardSpec.fold: 2x2 quad-fold grid centered at 250mm horizontally and vertically on the final trim.
boardSpec.path: Single linear S00-S43 dice path with no alternate routes.
boardSpec.pawnPads: Each board space has one separate printed pawn pad for a 6-8mm disc or cube; pawns must not cover the category icon.
boardSpec.hostTrackerPanel: Use a host tracker or blank QR box only. Do not print a fake QR code before the real hosted URL exists.
boardSpec.artMode: Hybrid raster/vector: high-resolution illustrated background under editable vector route, spaces, labels, icons, pawn pads, host tracker, legend, score reference, and setup rules.
boardSpec.productionStatus: Validated school prototype and manufacturer-brief package, not final commercial press artwork.

setup: Give each player one Starter Profile, one Player Board, one 6-8mm pawn disc or cube, and the fictional cash printed on their Starter Profile.
Place one pawn on S00 Student Start. If several players share a space, put one pawn on the pad and queue extras beside the tile in player-code order.
Shuffle the Investment, Market/Life, Ethics, Action, and Reflection decks separately. Put a face-up discard pile beside each deck.
Keep one D6, the fictional bank, and the shared price tracker near the host.
Use the QR web app when available to run the session, track prices, maintain player ledgers, score the game, sync session codes through the bundled backend, and export evidence.
turnSequence: Move forward on S00-S43. If a roll passes S43, stop at S43.
Record one decision, finance term, or evidence note on the Player Board.
Discard any drawn card face-up after it resolves.
rules.deckExhaustion: When a draw deck runs out, shuffle its discard pile into a new draw deck. If both draw deck and discard pile are empty, use the printed fallback for that space and continue.
rules.choiceTiming: Risky Choice effects that advance a pawn one extra space do not resolve the new space until that player's next turn.
rules.marketPrices: Asset price indexes cannot fall below 1.
rules.endCondition: Score when all players reach S43 Finish Review or after 12 turns per player, whichever happens first.
rules.evidenceBoundary: AI simulation verifies mechanics and balance. Real student surveys or playtests are required before claiming target-audience understanding.

scoringFormulas.portfolioValue: cash + sum(units held * current asset index * 1000)
scoringFormulas.portfolioScore: round(player portfolio value / highest player portfolio value * 25)
scoringFormulas.diversificationScore: min(20, unique asset categories * 4); if unique categories >= 5, score 20
scoringFormulas.riskManagementScore: min(15, riskEvidence * 2 + 4 if cash >= 20000 + 3 if unique categories >= 3)

boardSpaces.label: Student Start
boardSpaces.effect: Collect profile cash and choose first goal.

boardSpaces.label: Risk Decision
choices: Risky: advance +1 space after resolving this space; do not resolve the new space this turn.

boardSpaces.label: Cash Decision
choices: Chase return: advance +1 space after resolving this space; do not resolve the new space this turn.

boardSpaces.label: Impact Decision
choices: Profit sprint: advance +1 space after resolving this space; do not resolve the new space this turn.

starterProfiles.title: Budget Builder
starterProfiles.bonus: Start with +1 risk-management evidence if ending Turn 1 with INR 20000 cash.

starterProfiles.title: Opportunity Hunter
starterProfiles.bonus: First Growth or Index buy costs INR 2000 less.

starterProfiles.title: Ethical Analyst
starterProfiles.bonus: Start one step right on the ethics track.

starterProfiles.title: Balanced Planner
starterProfiles.bonus: If holding three categories by Turn 3, gain +2 reflection evidence.

starterProfiles.title: Trend Chaser
starterProfiles.bonus: Trend buy costs INR 1000 less, but first Trend loss costs one extra risk point.

investments.title: Emergency Cash
investments.text: Stable liquidity. Low return, high flexibility.

investments.title: School Savings Bond
investments.text: Defensive holding. Helps during market stress.

investments.title: GlobalBasket Index
investments.text: Connects personal investing to global systems.

investments.title: NovaTech Growth
investments.text: High upside; vulnerable to rate shocks.

ethics.title: Green Upgrade
ethics.prompt: Can lower short-term returns be justified?

ethics.title: Influencer Hype
ethics.prompt: What is the risk of promotion without evidence?

ethics.title: Data Misuse
ethics.prompt: How does privacy connect to investing?

ethics.title: Community Impact
ethics.prompt: Should community impact affect financial choice?

ethics.title: Carbon Fine
ethics.prompt: Can ESG risk become financial risk?

ethics.title: Fair Wage Vote
ethics.prompt: How do workers fit into economic value?

ethics.title: Greenwashing Alert
ethics.prompt: How can false sustainability claims mislead investors?

ethics.title: Long-Term Reputation
ethics.prompt: How can ethics protect future value?

actions.title: Research Report
actions.text: Peek at the next Market/Life Event.

actions.title: Due Diligence
actions.text: Buy Growth/Crypto/Trend at INR 2000 less after naming one risk.

actions.title: Stop-Loss
actions.text: Limit one risky asset loss this event to half.

actions.title: Insurance Hedge
actions.text: If a chosen category falls this round, gain INR 3000 cash.

actions.title: Cash Buffer
actions.text: If keeping INR 20000 cash after your turn, gain +2 risk evidence.

actions.title: Emergency Reserve
actions.text: If your portfolio fell and you hold Cash, gain INR 4000.

actions.title: Rebalance
actions.text: Sell one unit and buy a different category without penalty.

actions.title: Second Opinion
actions.text: Before a risky buy, name one risk and ask the host or a peer for a second opinion; clear reasoning earns +1 risk evidence.

actions.title: Peer Review
actions.text: Defend a decision with one finance term for +2 explanation evidence.

actions.title: Market Patience
actions.text: Choose not to trade; gain +1 risk evidence and INR 1000.

actions.title: Ethics Audit
actions.text: Responsible ethics choice moves one extra step right.

actions.title: Diversify Now
actions.text: If holding 1-2 categories, new category costs INR 2000 less.

reflection.title: Emotion Check
reflection.prompt: Name one decision you made because of emotion, not analysis. What should you have done instead?

reflection.title: Diversification
reflection.prompt: Explain your diversification strategy using at least one finance term.

reflection.title: Hidden Risk
reflection.prompt: Which asset looked attractive but carried hidden risk? Explain why.

reflection.title: Ethics Trade-Off
reflection.prompt: Describe one ethics decision and whether you prioritised return or responsibility.

reflection.title: Liquidity
reflection.prompt: When did holding cash help or hurt your strategy?

reflection.title: Event Risk
reflection.prompt: What market event changed your strategy the most?

reflection.title: Action Card
reflection.prompt: Which action card did you use, and how did it manage risk?

reflection.title: Replay
reflection.prompt: What would you change if you replayed from the start?

reflection.title: Global Systems
reflection.prompt: Explain one global event and how it affected personal investing choices.

reflection.title: Richest vs Best
reflection.prompt: Did the richest player also make the best decisions? Explain using score categories.

quickReference.title: Turn Order
quickReference.text: Roll one die, move on S00-S43, resolve the landing space, then log one decision.

quickReference.title: Finance Terms
quickReference.text: Volatility, diversification, liquidity, risk-return, FOMO, ESG.

quickReference.title: Choice Spaces
quickReference.text: Safe adds risk evidence, responsible adds ethics, risky may advance one extra space without resolving it this turn.

quickReference.title: Host Role
quickReference.text: Reveal events, track prices, export host evidence. Player boards hold portfolio records and final scoring.

qr.title: Host Tracker Setup
qr.text: Use the hosted dashboard only after a real URL is confirmed. Until then, use player boards and the price tracker.
